description_long: This is a hand-crafted, ancient-style ceramic bucket or small barrel with a wide, open mouth and a rounded base. The vessel features two integrated side handles, each formed from the same clay as the body, protruding horizontally for easy gripping. The exterior is decorated with a band of dark, teardrop-shaped markings encircling the upper portion, set against a lighter clay background, while the lower half is finished in a darker, reddish-brown tone. The surface is matte and slightly rough, with visible irregularities and texture from hand-molding. The interior is dark and unglazed, showing the natural clay material. This object is likely used for carrying or holding liquids or small items in a domestic or urban setting.
description: A hand-molded ceramic bucket with two side handles and decorative dark markings on a matte, textured surface.
description_view_0: The bucket is seen from an angle highlighting one prominent side handle and the interior's dark, rough texture.
description_view_1: This view shows both side handles, with the decorative band and teardrop markings clearly visible around the rim.
description_view_2: The bucket is viewed from the side, displaying both handles in profile and the full band of exterior markings.
description_view_3: The rear handle is most prominent, and the decorative band wraps around the vessel, with the base and body shape clearly visible.
